commitments.text: Consolidate Polaris brand page feedback into one master list
commitments.quote: Yeah, that's what I was going to do. I'll do that, and then by the end of today, there should be a thing within the deep dive and debate Quip.
commitments.context: You volunteered this unprompted — Brandon was about to suggest it and you beat him to it. The list becomes the artifact that proves you can run WW alignment. Thursday is the first test of the single-voice model.

commitments.text: Submit SIM to Alex for MX broken NB page images + Canada optimizations
commitments.quote: I'll be putting together that list of things that we would want so I can use some of that here.
commitments.context: Tomorrow's MX call is the first meeting with stakeholders since Carlos left. Having the SIM submitted lets you say "we have a ticket open" instead of "we plan to."

commitments.text: Email Lorena re: MX unallocated budget status
commitments.quote: I'm putting that down right now.
commitments.context: Brandon escalated this because Lorena hasn't responded to verbal asks. An email chain creates a paper trail Brandon can layer into if needed. Without it, year-end underspend risk sits untracked.

commitments.text: Create before/after slide for MX LP test
commitments.quote: Okay, so let's write this down. So you're going to submit a SIM today... and then create PowerPoint slide with before and after.
commitments.context: Brandon coached the exact visual: screenshot of old page (avoid broken images) beside the new Carlos page. Use wholesale page if beauty has broken images above the fold.

commitments.text: Follow up with LiveRamp — updated match rate + R&P ID requirement
commitments.quote: I'm going to ask if they can rerun that... there's also another point about enabling activation on Google Search for Amazon Business.
commitments.context: Remy already replied on the thread (4/14). The old analysis used 13% match rate — pre-associated emails. New analysis needed to reflect current state. Brandon is on the thread but hasn't seen the reply yet.

commitments.text: Walk team through Baloo interface next Thursday
commitments.quote: Yeah, I mean... I guess what's the thing that you would want them to take away from it?
commitments.context: You accepted this when Brandon asked. Format: live walkthrough + group bug-finding session. VM takes notes for SIM submission. This builds your credibility as the team's technical lead on new products.

commitments.text: Reply to Brandon — PAM budget needs assessment
commitments.quote: there's extra $ available in US. Do you need more $ for PAM?
commitments.context: Brandon offered extra US budget for PAM on 4/3. 11 days with no reply. He manages $2.8M in PS budget — when he offers money and gets silence, it signals disengagement. A 2-sentence Slack reply closes this.

commitments.text: Pull AU conversion rate data for Polaris LP change
commitments.quote: if you can pull that... the conversion rates... we wanted to be data led
commitments.context: Brandon asked for this to build the case for reverting Polaris in AU. Dwayne was about to make changes for Lena without data. This data is the leverage that keeps the decision in your hands, not Lena's.

commitments.text: Respond to Lena — AU LP URL analysis + CPA methodology
commitments.quote: Lena had reached out to Dwayne separately... we were waiting to email Lena and clarify what that go forward plan is
commitments.context: Lena is going around you to Dwayne. Brandon intervened to stop that. But the longer you don't respond, the more justified she feels going direct. The AU CVR data (commitment above) feeds directly into this response.

commitments.text: Coordinate VPN access for Adi — Canada OCI validation
commitments.quote: Richard, can you get access for Adi... that VPN tool
commitments.context: Adi needs Canada VPN for OCI validation step 3. Chantant team controls access. Quick coordination task — ask Chantant, CC Adi.

delegate.task: LiveRamp Enhanced Match — request updated match rate analysis and clarify R&P ID activation
delegate.reason: Vendor owns the data and runs the analysis. Richard asks questions, LiveRamp delivers. Copy Brandon to accelerate legal review.
delegate.quote: I just need to know the answer to those questions so that we can kick off the legal review
delegate.context: LiveRamp already replied to the thread (Remy Shrader, 4/14). The match rate analysis is their deliverable, not yours. Your job is to ask the right questions (R&P ID requirement, updated match rate post-associated-emails) and copy Brandon. Legal review is the gate — every day of delay pushes F90 Enhanced Match further into H2.

delegate.task: AU weekly WBR data pull and reporting
delegate.reason: Handoff doc drafted. AU team should own weekly reporting after transition week. Richard provides template and first walkthrough.
delegate.quote: I think that we should really have like one clean handoff
delegate.context: The AU handoff doc is drafted (au-handoff-rewrite.md). Brandon is reviewing today. After the handoff call, AU team owns weekly reporting. Your role shifts from executor to advisor — don't keep pulling the WBR data yourself after transition.

delegate.task: MX IECCP spend data input into finance file
delegate.reason: Stacey flagged Henry needs to run the report. Richard doesn't have finance system access.

delegate.task: Canada OCI validation step 3 — VPN access + final check
delegate.reason: Adi is running the validation. Richard's role is getting him access, not doing the validation.

delegate.task: Ad disapproval appeals and daily monitoring
delegate.reason: Andrew has the Google support relationship and is tracking the transient issue. Team just needs updates in group chat.

communicate.text: Brandon wants Richard to lead all global initiatives as single WW voice — no more fragmented stakeholder input on Polaris, LP testing, etc.
communicate.context: This is a scope expansion Brandon is handing you. Right now Adi, Yun, and others submit feedback to MCS directly. Brandon wants you to consolidate and be the single point of contact for all global LP/testing initiatives. Communicating this to the team isn't just informational — it's establishing your authority on WW testing. Do it in Thursday's Deep Dive when you present the Polaris feedback list.
communicate.quote: at the end of the day, it really should just be one voice, and that voice should be you

communicate.text: MX LP optimization call tomorrow 10 AM PT — Brandon joining. Frame as 3-part narrative: fixes, Carlos's test, Polaris rollout.
communicate.context: Yun is co-presenting tomorrow but doesn't know Brandon is joining or the 3-part messaging strategy (fixes + test + Polaris). A quick Slack DM to Yun tonight aligning on the narrative prevents surprises and shows you're coordinating, not just showing up.
communicate.quote: we definitely need to highlight... Richard's been doing all of this work

communicate.text: OCI Flash merging with regular flash — project status + performance only, no separate highlights/lowlights.
communicate.context: Weekly meeting (4/14). They write flash content — need to know the format change before next cycle.

communicate.text: Polaris brand pages: only US + JP should be live. Confirm no other markets applied.
communicate.context: Weekly meeting (4/14). Compliance check — if anyone applied Polaris outside US/JP, flag immediately.

communicate.text: AU handoff: Kate not concerned about HC. New L5 hire scope includes paid channels + experiments. Positioned as test, not takeover.
communicate.context: Brandon 1:1 (4/14). Team should understand AU org change and that PS retains advisory role.

communicate.text: Ad disapprovals across US/JP/CA — transient Google technical issue. Andrew leading appeals. Daily morning checks until resolved.
communicate.context: Weekly meeting (4/14). Monitor but don't panic.

differentiate.action: Ship Testing Approach doc to Brandon today
differentiate.why: Brandon (3/24): "That's why you're leading that testing document." Doc is done — PUBLISH 8.4/10. 14d overdue. 30 min of subtractive edits then send. Highest leverage action available.
differentiate.quote: That's why you're leading that testing document right now. But it's a balance — I don't want Richard spending 80% of his time writing documents.
differentiate.context: The doc passed critic review at 8.4/10 on 4/5. Ten days ago. The 5 remaining edits are all subtractive (removing words, not adding). Brandon explicitly said he'd review it the same day you send it. Every day you hold it is a day Brandon can't reference it in conversations with Kate. This is the single highest-leverage 30 minutes available to you today.

differentiate.action: Lead Polaris Deep Dive Thursday as single WW voice
differentiate.why: Brandon (4/14): "One voice, and that voice should be you... where we have these global initiatives, really want you to take the lead." Consolidate feedback list today, run the session Thursday.
differentiate.quote: where we have these global initiatives, really want you to kind of take the lead, maybe every time you can plan on having a deep dive
differentiate.context: Brandon is creating a recurring pattern: you own the Deep Dive slot for global initiatives. Thursday is the first test of this. The Polaris feedback list you're consolidating today becomes the artifact that proves you can run WW alignment. If this goes well, Brandon said he'll extend the time to an hour for future sessions. This is how WW Testing Lead becomes your actual title, not just a role.

differentiate.action: Frame MX LP call with 3-part narrative tomorrow
differentiate.why: Brandon (4/14) coached this exact framing: fixes + Carlos's test + Polaris rollout. "It really shows a lot of movement." Brandon joining to reinforce. Easy win — prep is 30 min.

differentiate.action: Ask Brandon explicitly about promo criteria and timeline
differentiate.why: Brandon (3/24): Rating stays "solid" until promo work begins. "Walk on water" reserved for active promo candidates. One conversation changes the trajectory.

differentiate.action: Proactively share AU/MX outcomes with stakeholders before they ask
differentiate.why: Brandon (3/24): "Proactive sharing of results is critical to elevate visibility." Also (4/14): "They only know what they are told." Artifacts > meeting updates.

differentiate.action: Kick off LiveRamp legal review — answer Brandon's Asana questions
differentiate.why: Brandon (4/14): "I'd rather kick off the legal review sooner versus later." F90 Enhanced Match is the OP1 play. Email to Remy takes 10 min.

differentiate.action: Write reusable wiki docs that teammates reference independently
differentiate.why: Brandon (3/24): "If Monami wants to reference best practices and you write it down in a Wiki page — cool." Artifacts others access = promo evidence.

differentiate.action: Follow team mechanisms consistently — change logs, deadlines, process
differentiate.why: Brandon (3/24): "Earning trust with them is feeding into those more regimented mechanisms." Growth area from annual review. Match the team's cadence.
